demoGuide.title: Scribe Guide
demoGuide.description: Interactive demo of every feature — formatting, wiki links, export, templates, and more.

appearance.themeDescription: Choose a default theme, generate a random one, or create your own.

storage.folderDescription: All documents are stored in the app's local database. This folder holds .scribe backups.
storage.folderNote: If disk writes fail, the editor keeps working and the document stays saved in the app. Change the folder or check disk permissions.
storage.whyAccessLink: Why does Scribe ask for folder access?
storage.reconcileSuccess: Synced: {{toDisk}} to disk, {{fromDisk}} from disk, {{imported}} new.
storage.clearDescription: Deletes all documents from the app, related .scribe files on disk, and stored images.
storage.clearConfirm: This permanently deletes all documents, .scribe files, and images in the folder. This action cannot be undone.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Quick actions available anywhere in the app.

storageAccess.title: Why does Scribe ask for folder access?
storageAccess.description: Explanation of Documents access and .scribe backup files
storageAccess.body1: Scribe saves documents first to its own in-app database. You can always write and edit — even without access to the Documents folder.
storageAccess.body2: It also creates backup .scribe files in a folder you choose (default ~/Documents/Scribe) so you can open them in Finder, back them up, or share them.
storageAccess.body3: macOS may show a system dialog asking for access to Documents or another location. This is normal — the system protects your files from apps.
storageAccess.body4: Scribe does not scan your entire disk. It only writes to the folder you select. If you deny access, the editor still works and you can change the folder later in Settings → Storage.

diskSync.warning: Writing the .scribe file to disk failed. The document is saved in the app. Check Settings → Storage.
